name_en: Institute for Political and International Studies
description: The Institute for Political and International Studies (IPIS) is the in-house think tank of Iran's [Ministry of Foreign Affairs](/orgs/ministry-of-foreign-affairs). Founded in 1983, it conducts research on foreign policy, regional affairs, and international relations, and trains diplomats. Under [Mostafa Zahrani](/people/mostafa-zahrani)'s directorship, IPIS oversaw the [Iran Experts Initiative](/events/iei-email-leak-2023), a covert influence operation that recruited Western-based Iranian academics to promote Tehran's nuclear negotiating positions in Western media and policy circles. [Saeed Khatibzadeh](/people/saeed-khatibzadeh), the initiative's mastermind, was appointed head of IPIS in January 2025.
editorial_notes: IPIS is sometimes referred to as the Foreign Ministry's think tank. Khatibzadeh appointed director in Jan 2025.
